title: Miconia borhidiana
taxonomy: Miconia borhidiana belongs to the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as the "melastome" or "memecyclid" family. This family is a group of flowering plants that are native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The genus Miconia, to which Miconia borhidiana belongs, is the largest and most diverse group within the Melastomataceae family. The species Miconia borhidiana is a part of the Melastomataceae family and is classified under the Melastomataceae order of plants. It is a member of the Ericales clade, which is an order of flowering plants that are known for their diverse range of species.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.
